source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Large Scale Infrastructure: Directory layout, toggling roles, and testing

questions.q: In the proposed layout, what is site.yml's role?
options: The orchestrator that includes the roles needed for the target nodes
questions.why: site.yml is the conductor: it includes the roles required for the targets.

questions.q: How is a role made optional (able to be disabled) in site.yml?
options: With a when condition using a default, e.g. when: enable_functionality1|default(true)
By commenting it out each time
questions.why: Include the role with when: enable_functionalityN|default(...) so it can be toggled per node.

options: The functionality is OFF unless a variable enables it
questions.why: default(false) leaves it disabled unless overridden to true.

questions.q: How do you turn functionality2 ON for one specific host?
options: Set enable_functionality2: true in that host's host_vars file
questions.why: Setting enable_functionality2: true in host_vars/<host>.yml enables it for that node only.

options: Passes an extra variable (here target=client1, which drives hosts: "{{ target }}")
Limits to a group named e
questions.why: -e supplies extra-vars; target selects which hosts the play runs against.

questions.q: Where are the roles referenced by site.yml expected to live?
options: Under a roles/ directory beside site.yml
questions.why: Roles live under roles/ at the same level as site.yml.

questions.q: A stated benefit of this organization is that...
options: Your systems' specifics are separated from code, auditable, and centralized in inventory files
It removes the need for SSH
It makes Ansible faster than Puppet
questions.why: The information-system specifics are separated from code, easy to audit, and centralized in the inventories.
